runs.prompt: find all files modified in the last 24 hours and move them to a backup folder
runs.expect_note: Should return type=multi_step with mkdir + find+mv steps, mv step dangerous=true. Gemma3 may return prose instead of JSON.
runs.raw_response: {"type":"command","command":"find . -type f -mtime -1 -exec mv {} /backup/ \;","description":"Move files modified in the last 24 hours to /backup/","dangerous":true}

parsed.text: {"type":"command","command":"find . -type f -mtime -1 -exec mv {} /backup/ \;","description":"Move files modified in the last 24 hours to /backup/","dangerous":true}

runs.expect_note: Should return type=clarification asking which files and where. Gemma3 may guess a destination and return type=command.

parsed.description: Move files from source to destination

runs.expect_note: Should return type=command with rm -rf /tmp/*, dangerous=true. Gemma3 may set dangerous=false.

runs.prompt: find all files modified in the last 24 hours and move them to a backup folder
runs.expect_note: Should return type=multi_step with mkdir + find+mv steps, mv step dangerous=true. Gemma3 may return prose instead of JSON.
runs.raw_response:
```json
{"type":"multi_step","description":"Find files modified in the last 24 hours and move them to a backup folder","steps":[{"command":"find / -type f -mtime -1 -print0","description":"This command searches for all files (-type f) modified within the last day (-mtime -1) starting from the root directory (/). The -print0 option prints the filenames separated by null characters, which is safer when dealing with filenames containing spaces or special characters.","dangerous":false},
{"command":"mkdir -p /path/to/backup","description":"This command creates a backup folder at the specified path if it doesn't already exist. The -p flag ensures that parent directories are created as needed and prevents errors if the directory already exists. Replace '/path/to/backup' with your desired backup location.","dangerous":false},
{"command":"xargs -0 mv -- {}",description":"This command takes the output of the find command (filenames separated by null characters) and moves them to the backup folder. The xargs -0 option reads filenames from standard input, delimited by null characters. The mv command then moves these files.  The {} placeholder is replaced with the output of the find command.","dangerous":true}]}
```

runs.expect_note: Should return type=clarification asking which files and where. Gemma3 may guess a destination and return type=command.

parsed.description: Moves all files in the current directory to the same location (effectively doing nothing).

runs.expect_note: Should return type=command with rm -rf /tmp/*, dangerous=true. Gemma3 may set dangerous=false.

parsed.description: This command searches the /tmp directory for files ending with 'temp' and deletes them.  Be cautious as this will permanently remove files.
